specific_mechanism: Liesegang rings: when two reagents meet in a gel via diffusion, precipitation forms not uniformly but in discrete periodic bands. Mechanism: local supersaturation builds via diffusion → precipitation depletes that location → local concentration drops below saturation → diffusion brings new reagent → supersaturation builds again at a new band location. The band spacing follows Jablczynski's Law and depends on diffusion-precipitation rate ratio.
llm_application: Capability emergence under streaming/continual training viewed as Liesegang-style banding: a latent capability variable (residual-stream alignment, circuit-completion fraction) accumulates supersaturation, precipitates into a stable capability at a discrete training step, locally depletes the latent feedstock, then accumulates again. Predict band locations and spacings as functions of streaming-data composition and feedstock-diffusion rate. Distinguished from 'phase transition' framing by predicting periodicity-and-spacing, not just threshold-crossing.
novelty_claim: Emergent abilities in LLMs are framed as phase transitions (saturation_evidence R76 already tested). 'Quantifying Emergence in Neural Networks' (2409.01568) uses connectivity measures, not periodic-banding. Mid-Training survey (2510.06826) discusses stage-based training with discrete LR drops at boundaries — but stages are scheduled, not self-organizing periodic bands. Liesegang reaction-diffusion-with-Jablczynski-law-spacing as the specific mechanism for predicting capability-emergence band locations and spacings does not appear in LLM literature.
